title: Michigan Court Abbreviation Catalogue
description: A comprehensive catalogue of Michigan state and federal court abbreviations, administrative authority codes, and case type classification codes.

categories.name: Michigan State Court Abbreviations
categories.description: Abbreviations for courts within Michigan's 'One Court of Justice' system, as established by the Michigan Constitution of 1963, Article VI.

entries.description: The highest court in Michigan. It has superintending control over all courts in the state, has final appellate jurisdiction, and issues Michigan Supreme Court opinions cited as 'Mich.'
An intermediate appellate court that hears appeals from the circuit courts, the probate courts, and certain administrative agencies. Opinions are cited as 'Mich. App.'
The highest trial court in Michigan, with general original jurisdiction over all civil claims over $25,000, felony criminal cases, family law matters, and appeals from lower courts.

Often called the 'people's court', handles civil claims up to $25,000, misdemeanors, civil infractions, landlord-tenant disputes, and small claims. The most frequently used court by Michigan residents.
Handles estates, trusts, mental health proceedings, guardianships, conservatorships, and matters involving minors. Has exclusive jurisdiction over the administration of decedents' estates.

Retained by a few cities such as Grosse Pointe. These courts predate the district court system and continue to operate under local authority, handling local ordinance violations and limited civil matters.
entries.note: Most municipal courts were replaced by the district court system established in 1968.

entries.description: An independent body that investigates complaints against judges in Michigan. The JTC has the authority to recommend discipline, including removal from office, to the Michigan Supreme Court.

categories.name: Federal Court Abbreviations
categories.description: Abbreviations for federal courts located in or whose jurisdiction affects Michigan.

entries.description: The highest court in the federal judiciary and the final authority on federal constitutional questions. Its decisions are binding on all courts throughout the United States.

entries.full_name: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Sixth Circuit
entries.description: The federal intermediate appellate court that covers Michigan, Ohio, Kentucky, and Tennessee. Decisions are binding on the federal district courts within those states.

entries.full_name: U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of Michigan
entries.description: The federal trial court for the eastern portion of Michigan, including Detroit, Flint, Ann Arbor, Saginaw, and surrounding areas. One of two federal district courts in Michigan.
entries.citation_example: Doe v. Roe, 456 F. Supp. 3d 789 (E.D. Mich. 2021)

entries.full_name: U.S. District Court for the Western District of Michigan
entries.description: The federal trial court for the western portion of Michigan, including Grand Rapids, Lansing, Kalamazoo, Muskegon, Marquette, and surrounding areas.
entries.citation_example: Doe v. Roe, 789 F. Supp. 3d 123 (W.D. Mich. 2021)

categories.name: Court Rule & Administrative Authorities
categories.description: Abbreviations identifying the primary authorities and rule sets governing the Michigan 'One Court of Justice' system.

entries.description: The principal set of procedural rules governing practice and procedure in all Michigan courts. Promulgated by the Michigan Supreme Court, these rules cover pleadings, motions, discovery, trials, and appeals.
entries.scope: General procedure in all Michigan courts

entries.description: The rules governing the admissibility of evidence in Michigan courts. Modeled in part on the Federal Rules of Evidence, the MRE controls what evidence may be presented to the trier of fact at trial.
entries.scope: Evidentiary standards in all Michigan courts

entries.description: The administrative arm of the Michigan Supreme Court, responsible for providing administrative support to the Michigan court system, collecting and publishing court statistics, and developing standard court forms.
entries.scope: Statewide court administration and standardized forms

entries.description: The ethical rules governing the professional conduct of attorneys licensed in Michigan. Violations may result in discipline by the Attorney Grievance Commission and the Attorney Discipline Board.
entries.enforcement_body: Attorney Grievance Commission / Attorney Discipline Board

entries.description: The ethical standards governing the conduct of all judges in Michigan, including standards for impartiality, integrity, and the avoidance of impropriety. Enforced through the Judicial Tenure Commission.

categories.name: Case Type Classification Codes
categories.description: Every Michigan case number ends with a two-letter alpha code indicating the court's jurisdiction and the nature of the case. These codes are assigned by the State Court Administrative Office (SCAO).

codes.label: Administrative Agencies
codes.description: Appeals from decisions of state and local administrative agencies, including licensing boards and regulatory bodies.

codes.label: Criminal Appeals
codes.description: Appeals of criminal convictions or sentences from the district court to the circuit court.

codes.label: Civil Appeals
codes.description: Appeals of civil judgments from the district court or probate court to the circuit court.

codes.label: Housing
codes.description: Circuit-level housing matters, including appeals from district court housing cases or circuit-jurisdiction housing issues.

codes.label: Divorce with Minor Children
codes.description: Domestic relations cases involving divorce or separate maintenance where there are minor children, requiring determinations of custody, parenting time, and child support.

codes.label: Felony
codes.description: Criminal cases involving felony-level offenses. Circuit courts have original jurisdiction over all felony proceedings in Michigan.

codes.label: General Civil
codes.description: General civil claims filed in district court, typically for money damages up to the jurisdictional limit of $25,000.

codes.label: Landlord-Tenant
codes.description: Summary proceedings for possession of residential or commercial property, including eviction (summary proceedings) and related claims.

codes.label: Small Claims
codes.description: Informal proceedings for civil money claims up to $7,000 (or $10,000 for sole proprietors) where attorneys generally may not represent parties.

codes.label: Statute Misdemeanor
codes.description: Criminal cases involving state-law misdemeanor offenses punishable by up to one year in jail.

codes.label: Statute Civil Infraction
codes.description: Non-criminal civil infraction violations, including traffic offenses, where no jail time may be imposed.

codes.label: Supervised Estates
codes.description: Formal, court-supervised administration of a decedent's estate, requiring court approval of all significant steps from inventory through final distribution.

codes.label: Unsupervised Estates
codes.description: Independent (unsupervised) administration of a decedent's estate, where the personal representative acts without routine court oversight, subject to a final accounting.

codes.label: Mental Illness
codes.description: Proceedings related to the involuntary hospitalization or treatment of individuals with serious mental illness, including petitions for clinical certificates and hospitalization orders.

codes.label: Judicial Admission
codes.description: Voluntary admission proceedings for individuals who consent to mental health treatment under judicial oversight, as distinguished from involuntary commitment.

categories.name: Judicial Bias Detection & Disqualification Strategy
categories.description: Advanced strategies for detecting, documenting, and challenging judicial bias — bias pattern recognition, building a disqualification record, MCR 2.003 motion checklist, and key caselaw including Cain, Caperton, Liteky, and In re MKK.

categories.name: PPO Fraud & Perjury — Offensive Challenges
categories.description: Offensive strategies to challenge PPOs obtained through fraud, perjury, or material misrepresentation — fraud-on-the-court framework (MCR 2.612(C)(1)(c)), perjury statutes (MCL 750.422–425), impeachment tools, abuse-of-process and malicious-prosecution claims, and weaponized-PPO indicators.

categories.name: Contempt Defense — Advanced Strategies
categories.description: Advanced contempt defense including threshold defenses (inability, ambiguity, void order), show cause hearing procedural requirements checklist, appellate standards of review, and emergency relief from contempt incarceration.

categories.name: Parental Alienation & Custody Manipulation
categories.description: Legal framework for parental alienation in Michigan — MCL 722.23 best-interest factors, alienation recognition and documentation, custody evaluations and GAL tools, and key caselaw including Pierron, Vodvarka, and Berger.

categories.name: Prosecutorial & Court Staff Misconduct
categories.description: Remedies for prosecutorial misconduct (Brady violations, vindictive/selective prosecution), FOC staff misconduct (biased recommendations, ex parte communications), court staff/clerk misconduct, and consolidated remedies table.

categories.name: Gender Bias in Family Court
categories.description: Constitutional and ethical framework for challenging gender bias — equal protection (US Const amend XIV; Mich Const art I, §2), bias indicators specific to family law, documentation strategies, and legal vehicles including MCR 2.003, JTC complaint, and 42 USC §1983.

categories.name: Cumulative Due Process Violations
categories.description: Framework for arguing cumulative due process error — expanded Mathews v Eldridge analysis, cumulative error doctrine, comprehensive violation inventory checklist, and appellate presentation strategy.

categories.name: Remedies & Strategic Vehicles
categories.description: Master remedies table consolidating all available vehicles — PPO termination, relief from judgment, disqualification, contempt relief, appeal, superintending control, habeas corpus, JTC/AGC complaints, 42 USC §1983 — with strategic sequencing, immunity analysis, and §1983 in family law context.

categories.name: Family Court Fraud, Perjury & Structural Bias — Appellate Relief
categories.description: Consolidated strategy for family-court fraud/perjury, court-employee conflicts, judicial refusal/overreach, appellate and extraordinary relief (appeal/stay/superintending control), structural court-capture indicators, and fathers' rights preservation.

related_files.title: Michigan Court Rules (MCR) — Complete Rule Index
related_files.description: All Michigan Court Rules organized by chapter (1–9) with rule numbers, titles, and summaries.

related_files.title: Michigan Rules of Evidence (MRE) — Complete Rule Index
related_files.description: All Michigan Rules of Evidence organized by article (I–XI) with rule numbers, titles, summaries, and federal counterparts.

related_files.title: Michigan Compiled Laws (MCL) — Court-Practice Reference
related_files.description: Key MCL sections organized by chapter covering courts (RJA), estates (EPIC), family law, criminal procedure, mental health, torts, business organizations, and juvenile law.

related_files.title: Michigan Court Rules — Human-Readable Reference
related_files.description: Formatted, human-readable reference for all Michigan Court Rules.

related_files.description: Formatted, human-readable reference for all Michigan Rules of Evidence.

related_files.description: Formatted, human-readable reference for key MCL court-practice sections.
